content.full_text:
July 19, 1976

Mr. Thomas N. Saunders
4141 Twentieth Street
San Francisco, California 94114

Dear Mr. Saunders:

Thank you for your letter of July 12, 1976, regarding the confirmation letter you received in connection with an audit of the campaign statement of the Floyd Mori Campaign Committee.

Since you indicate in your letter that you are in favor of the concept of campaign disclosure, you are probably aware that in the past, disclosure laws were more often than not ignored, largely because of a general lack of enforcement. Experience to date indicates that the knowledge on the part of candidates and campaign committees that their statements will be audited is a very effective enforcement device. At the present time, most candidates file campaign statements that appear to be complete and accurate.

With respect to the method of conducting the audit, which you criticize, you are probably aware that the audits are carried out by the Franchise Tax Board, not by the Fair Political Practices Commission. I would assume that the Board does not expect contributors to conduct extraordinary searches of their records; and that the information you provided is adequate to meet their needs. Certainly, you are correct when you say that the law does not require you to furnish detailed information. Nevertheless, the cooperation of yourself and other contributors will help assure that campaign disclosure continues to be a reality and not merely an idea.

I am sending a copy of your letter to the Franchise Tax Board so that they may consider your suggestions. I appreciate your interest.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

June First 19d[illegible]

THOMAS N. SAUNDERS

4141 TWENTIETH STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94114
TELEPHONE (415) 697-0500

July 12, 1976

Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir:

While I readily admit to having been fairly loud in my opposition to the passage of Proposition 9 in 1974, I favored then and continue to favor the concept of "disclosure". However, I think the procedure for auditing campaign reports, as evidenced by the attached form, puts an unreasonable burden on small contributors like myself, goes beyond what the law requires, and will certainly not encourage continued giving by small contributors.

As I read this audit form, I am asked to report the date of my contribution or contributions within an eight-month period, the amount and whether by cash or check.

To comply with that request would require that I ferret out from the basement last year's financial records and go through all check stubs (my wife and I maintain four different checking accounts) to develop the facts you request. There is nothing in the law that requires that I furnish this type of detailed information.

I am perfectly willing to confirm that I did contribute to a given campaign and if you will tell me the amount the recipient reported as my contribution I will attest that to the best of my recollection that figure is or is not correct. This is the system commonly "used by" auditors and seems adequate for your purposes.

There were a couple of good ideas in Proposition 9. I hope that over-zealous enforcement won't destroy them.

Sincerely,

[signature]

cc: Political Reform Audit Program


[illegible]

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

POLITICAL REFORM AUDIT PROGRAM
745 FRANKLIN STREET, SUITE 308
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94102
TELEPHONE (415) 557-0403

July 8, 1976


Thomas N. Saunders
4141 - 20th St.
San Francisco, CA  94114


The voters of California enacted the Political Reform Act in the June 1974 election and by so doing added audits as a new dimension to the political process. A confirmation letter, such as this, is sent to campaign contributors, as a necessary and routine part of an audit, to verify monetary and nonmonetary contributions reported. This procedure is applied to all candidates, officers, and committees that are subject to audit under the Act.  No special significance should be attached to either our sending, or your receiving, this confirmation letter.

Your name is shown as a contributor on campaign statements filed by

Floyd Mori Campaign Committee__________________________, for the period

May 2, 1975_________________ through ___December 31, 1975_________________.

Your confirmation of contributions made to the above, by furnishing the information requested below, will assist the department in the completion of its audit.

A postage-paid envelope is enclosed for your reply.


Political Reform Audit

Attach.                                                      Other
        Date          Check           Cash             Value*            Description

_____________  I did contribute. Do not recollect date &
_____________  ________________  ________________  amount__________________

_____________  ________________  ________________  ________________  ________________

*If nonmonetary, enter estimated fair market value.
                                            Signature: Hannah Saunders______________

                                            Date:      July 12, 1976________________

(If more space is needed for listing, use reverse side of this form, or attach a
separate schedule.)

FTB 5220-40 (6-76)
